system.docHtml: Shock represents a combatant's physiological resilience to the trauma of violent injury—the ability to absorb a blow without being incapacitated by pain, blood loss, or the body's involuntary shutdown responses. It is tested whenever a character suffers significant physical damage in combat, determining whether they can continue to fight or are overcome by the shock of their wounds. Conditioning, body mass, and raw endurance all contribute to this capacity, which can mean the difference between staying on one's feet and collapsing from a wound that a tougher individual might shrug off.
